text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet little town named Serenity Springs, there lived a gentle owl named Orion. He wasn’t like any owl you’d ever meet; instead, he hatched from a magic egg that glowed like the stars. Orion had a curiosity that drove him beyond silent nights. One evening, as he glided through the tall pines, he heard a peculiar tapping sound coming from an old oak tree at the edge of the town.

It was a girl, Emily. She lived alone in a cottage just beyond the old oak, reading by the light of her spinning wheel. But what caught Orion’s attention wasn’t her solitude—it was her nightly tapping.

Each evening, as the sun set, she’d tap, tapping on the oak. Orion didn’t know why. Was she talking to the tree?

One autumn evening, Orion decided to visit. He didn’t know why—it just seemed right. As he flew nearer, he noticed she wasn’t tapping; she was
